analysis.summary: The MeridianSecurityToken contract demonstrates strong ERC-1400 compliance with proper implementation of partition-based transfers, document management, operator controls, and controller functions. Minor issues exist around partition tracking for holders and the limited scope of forced transfers to only the UNLOCKED_PARTITION, but core security token functionality is correctly implemented.

results.finding: Contract correctly implements canTransferByPartition with proper ESC codes (0x51, 0x52, 0x54, 0x57) and transferByPartition enforces LOCKED_PARTITION restriction. The _transferByPartition internal function properly validates balances and prevents transfers from locked partitions.

Document management is fully implemented with setDocument (controller-only) storing URI, hash, and timestamp, getDocument returning all three values per ERC-1400 spec, and getAllDocuments providing document enumeration. DocumentUpdated event is properly emitted.

Comprehensive operator system implemented with authorizeOperator/revokeOperator for global operators and authorizeOperatorByPartition/revokeOperatorByPartition for partition-scoped operators. The isOperator and isOperatorForPartition functions correctly check authorization hierarchy including controller privileges.

results.rule: Forced transfer capability for regulatory compliance
results.finding: controllerTransfer and controllerRedeem are implemented with proper onlyController modifier and isControllable check. However, controllerTransfer only operates on UNLOCKED_PARTITION, which may limit regulatory intervention capability for locked tokens requiring forced movement.

results.rule: Issuance controls — only authorized controllers can mint
results.finding: issueByPartition correctly restricted to controller via onlyController modifier, checks isIssuable flag, and renounceIssuance allows permanent disabling of minting. Proper events (IssuedByPartition, TransferByPartition) are emitted on issuance.

redeemByPartition allows self-redemption and operatorRedeemByPartition properly validates operator authorization via isOperatorForPartition. Both functions correctly decrement totalSupply and emit RedeemedByPartition events. No partition restrictions on redemption allows flexibility.

recommendations: Extend controllerTransfer to accept a partition parameter, enabling regulatory forced transfers from LOCKED_PARTITION when required for compliance actions
Implement _partitionsOf tracking in issuance and transfer functions to enable partitionsOf(address) enumeration as specified in ERC-1400
Add a function to retrieve all partitions held by a token holder (partitionsOf) which is currently defined but never populated
Consider adding a controllerTransferByPartition function that allows the controller to move tokens from any partition for full regulatory compliance capability
Add input validation to prevent self-authorization in authorizeOperatorByPartition similar to the check in authorizeOperator
